records.ety: [L. expediens, -entis, p. pr. of expedire to be expedient, release, extricate: cf. F. expdient. See Expedite.]

defs: Hastening or forward; hence, tending to further or promote a proposed object; fit or proper under the circumstances; conducive to self-interest; desirable; advisable; advantageous; -- sometimes contradistinguished from right or principled.
quotes: It is expedient for you that I go away.
Nothing but the right can ever be expedient, since that can never be true expediency which would sacrifice a greater good to a less.
His marches are expedient to this town.

defs: That which serves to promote or advance; suitable means to accomplish an end.
quotes: What sure expedient than shall Juno find, To calm her fears and ease her boding mind?

defs: Means devised in an exigency; shift.
